title: Luffy Decides to Free Zoro
description: On pages 11–13, this part moves through Shells Town and the first clues about Zoro, Morgan, and Helmeppo’s abuse of power.
scene: In Shells Town, Luffy, Coby, and Zoro are drawn into the cruelty of Morgan’s rule and Helmeppo’s privilege.

dialogue.speaker: Helmeppo
dialogue.text: I'LL TELL MY FATHER ON YOU!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: HE MUST BE SOME HIGH RANKING NAVAL OFFICER.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Now that little girl will be safe.

dialogue.speaker: Helmeppo
dialogue.text: Well... if it isn't the IDIOT SON, living off Daddy's wealth.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Did you just call ME AN "IDIOT"?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: A MAN IN YOUR POSITION SHOULD GUARD HIS TONGUE... OR HE COULD LOSE IT!

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: Did you bring those rice balls for me? How thought- full.

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: Disgusting! It's sweet! There's sugar on it!

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: Rice balls are supposed to be seasoned with salt! Not sugar!!

dialogue.speaker: Helmeppo
dialogue.text: B-BUT I LIKE SWEETS, SO I figured Sugar would be better!

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: HOW EVIL... THAT LITTLE GIRL WORKED REALLY HARD TO MAKE THOSE RICE BALLS.

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: Don't worry! I'm sure the ants find them delicious!

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: WHY!!! I worked so hard to make them!!

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: Anyone Biding this prisoner shall be found guilty of the crimes he has committed. Captain Morgan

dialogue.speaker: Morgan
dialogue.text: It says, "Anyone adding this prisoner shall be found guilty of the crimes he has committed.

summary: The Shells Town conflict uses Zoro’s imprisonment and Morgan’s rule to test what kind of people Luffy chooses to trust. The section moves Zoro closer to becoming Luffy’s first crewmate.
